briefing.opening: Good morning. We will now begin the climate briefing for March 25, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Change: Global Crisis Signs and Expanding Domestic Impacts
sections.content: The impacts of climate change are intensifying globally, with various signs of crisis being observed both domestically and internationally. Domestically, climate change is causing earlier mosquito appearances and shifts in mosquito distribution, leading to responses such as the activation of AI-based surveillance systems in Jeju and Sejong [1, 13, 19]. Concerns are also rising regarding agricultural damage, including the outbreak of fruit tree borers and the spread of rice blast disease [14, 15]. Internationally, extreme phenomena are being reported, such as record-breaking March heatwaves across the United States and the rapid sinking of Jakarta, Indonesia, due to climate change and overdevelopment [106, 109, 111, 119]. Furthermore, observations include earlier but rapid melting of winter snow, increased spread of dangerous Aspergillus fungi, and changes in the population of the Large Heath butterfly, indicating ecological disruptions. Impacts on human health, such as increased anxiety and depression, are also being raised [114, 115, 117, 121]. Recently, in the Lincolnshire region of the UK, homes have been inundated by floods, demonstrating how the direct impacts of climate change are threatening citizens' lives [126].

sections.title: ⚠️ Challenges in Carbon Neutrality Policy Implementation and Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: The implementation of carbon neutrality policies and the transition to renewable energy still face numerous challenges and difficulties. Domestically, difficulties in policy promotion have emerged, such as the mass resignation of the agenda deliberation group during the parliamentary public discourse process for carbon neutrality, disrupting the deliberation process [21, 43, 104]. Additionally, concerns have been raised that Incheon's carbon neutrality master plan lacks sufficient reduction effects [24]. In rural areas, there is a growing call for the government to establish a fair compensation system for the carbon neutrality contributions of agricultural and fishing communities [27, 32, 45]. In the renewable energy sector, a series of accidents have occurred with aging wind turbines, including a fatal fire at a Yeongdeok wind farm [52, 95, 105]. This emphasizes the need to strengthen safety management, such as mandating safety evaluations for renewable energy facilities that have been in operation for over 20 years [51, 56, 62, 73, 90]. Internationally, signs of policy setbacks are also being detected, with a German minister mentioning a potential relaxation of the EU's carbon neutrality targets, and the Maryland Supreme Court in the U.S. dismissing a local government's climate change damage compensation lawsuit, ruling in favor of a fuel company [26, 113, 122].

sections.title: 🌱 Efforts by Domestic Companies and Local Communities in Climate Change Response
sections.content: Despite these challenges, domestic companies and local communities are making various efforts to respond to climate change and achieve carbon neutrality. ESG management is expanding among companies, with a domestic steel company being recognized as a leader in climate change response, and KT&G being selected as an excellent company in CDP's climate change and water management sectors for four consecutive years [3, 4, 9, 16, 17]. Samsung C&T has completed the nation's first green hydrogen production facility utilizing 100% renewable energy, and Hyundai Wia has announced its commitment to transition 100% of its electricity consumption to renewable energy, leading the clean energy transition [48, 49, 57, 64, 71, 79, 80, 82, 84, 91, 92, 96, 98, 100, 102, 103]. In the agricultural sector, the Rural Development Administration is developing overwintering techniques for honeybees and responding to the climate change crisis through smart farming technologies [2, 7, 8, 11, 20]. The Ministry of Oceans and Fisheries is supporting small and medium-sized shipping companies in their transition to eco-friendly vessels, demonstrating ongoing adaptation and mitigation efforts across various sectors [29, 39]. Furthermore, various activities encouraging participation from local governments and civil society are actively being promoted, including Cheongju City's acceleration of carbon neutrality policies, Yeosu City's Arbor Day tree-planting event, Wanju County's carbon neutrality digital literacy education and declaration of a 'Sunshine Income Basic Society,' and Seongbuk-gu's environmental love drawing competition [31, 34, 35, 36, 41, 44, 46, 47, 55, 61, 65, 74, 76, 77, 85, 88, 94].

sections.title: 🌍 Strengthening International Climate Action and Cooperation
sections.content: The international community is also strengthening cooperation and action to confront the threat of climate change. South Korea and China held a joint committee on climate change cooperation for the first time in seven years, agreeing to strengthen efforts for air quality improvement and expanding regional coordination [5, 6, 10, 12, 18, 123]. New York State in the U.S. is mandating climate change education to raise awareness among future generations [107], and the search for scientifically-based solutions is active, with global research identifying urgent blue carbon priorities for climate change response [108]. The UK is strengthening energy independence by mandating the installation of solar panels and heat pumps in new homes [93], and Brazil is pursuing concrete national climate actions, announcing the acceleration of climate action projects in over 30 cities and regions through its 'Mutirão' program [124]. Meanwhile, private sector transition efforts are also noteworthy, with major Indian corporations accelerating solar transitions and continued investment in commercial renewable energy companies [86]. Realistic challenges in the energy transition process are also being transparently shared, such as the Australian Minister for Climate Change and Energy disclosing the status of fuel-depleted gas stations within the country [125].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlights that while the threat of climate change is becoming a reality, diverse domestic and international efforts are underway to overcome it. This is a critical time that demands stronger and more consistent climate action for a sustainable future. This concludes today's climate briefing.
